ogTitle: Devon North vs Dhurringile: Climate Safety

heading: Devon North vs Dhurringile — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Devon North, Victoria and Dhurringile, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Devon North has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Dhurringile (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Devon North or Dhurringile safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Devon North has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Dhurringile scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Devon North scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Dhurringile scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Devon North or Dhurringile?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Devon North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
